commit.message:
Fix party expense log pagination and loading loops (#133)

* fix(pwa): stabilize party expense log pagination

Summary:
- extract party expense pagination into a dedicated library with
  imperative loaders, stable snapshots, and newest-first expense
  ordering
- replace the buggy hook implementation with a Suspense-friendly
  wrapper and preload the visible expense chunks from the party route
- add unit and Playwright coverage for chunk pagination, live updates,
  and newly created expenses returning to the top of the log

Rationale:
- the previous hook could trigger infinite update loops and was hard to
  reuse safely for preloading and Suspense integration
- moving the pagination state outside the hook makes the loading model
  predictable and gives us focused tests around chunk behavior

Tests:
- pnpm lingui:extract
- pnpm lint
- pnpm typecheck
- pnpm test
- pnpm --filter @trizum/pwa exec playwright test e2e/expense-log.spec.ts
  --project=chromium --workers=1 --reporter=line
- PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=http://localhost:5173 pnpm --filter @trizum/pwa
  exec playwright test e2e/expense-log.spec.ts --project=chromium
  --workers=1 --reporter=line

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(pwa): stop resuspending the expense log after mount

Summary:
- remove the local ExpenseLog Suspense boundary from the party route
- keep already loaded expenses visible while new leading chunks load in
  the background
- make the expense pagination hook non-suspending and extend the unit
  coverage for leading-chunk updates

Rationale:
- once the party route has mounted we do not want new synced chunks to
  bounce the whole screen back into a pending state
- the route loader already preloads the visible expense page, so the
  hook can safely switch to background loading for follow-up updates

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @trizum/pwa exec vitest run src/lib/partyPaginatedExpenses.test.ts
- pnpm --filter @trizum/pwa typecheck
- pnpm --filter @trizum/pwa exec eslint src/hooks/usePartyPaginatedExpenses.ts src/lib/partyPaginatedExpenses.ts src/lib/partyPaginatedExpenses.test.ts 'src/routes/party.$partyId.tsx'
- PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=http://localhost:5173 pnpm --filter @trizum/pwa exec playwright test e2e/expense-log.spec.ts --project=chromium --workers=1 --reporter=line

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Signed-off-by: GitHub Actions <actions@github.com>

commit.message:
fix(feed): add missing stock_quantity and manage_stock to GMC feed query (#851)

The Google Merchant Center feed route was selecting only the legacy
`stock` column, ignoring `stock_quantity` and `manage_stock`. This
caused products with manage_stock=false to appear out-of-stock (instead
of unlimited/9999) and products using the modern stock_quantity field to
fall back to the legacy stock value (often 0).

Extracts the select projection into a testable constant in feed-query.ts
and adds route-level regression tests that capture the actual select
argument and assert both columns are present.

refactor: use shift-by-1 linearity for Euler-form descent

with the actual base assumption `[(shiftFunctor C (1 : ℤ)).Linear k]`,
using the existing `shiftFunctor_linear` instance to recover linearity for

  `eulerFormInner_isTriangleAdditive`, `eulerForm`, and

- simplify `bridgelandCorollary_1_3_complexManifold` to use the same base

commit.message:
Harden review aggregate against stale older rounds

Restrict review aggregate to the current active round and serialize review
state mutations so stale rounds cannot clobber newer review state during
concurrent start or aggregate flows.

Also add package-level regression coverage, archive the tracked plan, and
record follow-up issues for explicit round supersede and historical backfill
work.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Migrate board event identity to author/session semantics

Move board event identity from the legacy userId-only model to
explicit authorUserId + sessionId semantics across the microboard
library surface.

This updates local event creation to emit authorUserId and sessionId,
keeps userId only as a compatibility mirror/fallback, and migrates
self-echo detection plus undo/redo ownership to session-scoped logic.
Batch expansion and replay now preserve the new identity fields, while
legacy records that only carry body.userId continue to resolve as old
session-like origins during the migration.

Presence handling is also moved to session-scoped identity. Remote
presence actors are keyed by session, no longer collapsed by shared
account identity, and transport-facing presence message types now carry
sessionId and authorUserId metadata.

Tests cover same-author different-session remote behavior, legacy
body.userId fallback, outbound batching metadata, and session-scoped
undo ownership. Build and focused test runs pass after the migration.

Implement websocket board session handshake in UI transport

Persist the active board-editor session in the microboardUI connection layer and send x-board-session-id on reconnect so the server can resume the same logical editor session.

Adopt sessionId and author identity from connect/auth responses, expose them back through the Connection implementation via sessionId/authorUserId and getSessionId()/getAuthorUserId(), and keep presence messages aligned with the new session-scoped identity model.

fix: restore GPU CI job (#23)

## Summary
- keep the GPU CI on the archived two-stage flow while removing the
stale bellman-cuda / CRS setup
- fix archived integration tests to resolve `test_fixtures` and
`artifacts/app.bin` from the runtime `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR` that nextest
remaps on the GPU runner
- remove the stale `WORKSPACE_DIR` override from the archive build step
because it was not actually fixing archive replay

## Validation
- `cargo fmt -p ethereum_prover -- --check`
- `ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=true cargo nextest list -p ethereum_prover
--test integration --release`
- `ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=true cargo nextest archive --release -p
ethereum_prover --test integration --archive-file
prover-gpu-tests-local.tar.zst`
- `cargo nextest run --archive-file prover-gpu-tests-local.tar.zst
--workspace-remap "$(pwd)" cpu_witness_from_fixture_block`

## Context
The GPU CI failure was not missing fixture files in the workflow
checkout. The archived test binary was using compile-time
`env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")`, so after being built in the container it
still pointed at the container path when replayed on the GPU runner.
Switching the test helper to the runtime `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR` makes the
archive replay pick up the remapped GPU-runner checkout path as
intended.

* refactor(cli-agent): prefer submitted commands over raw chunks

Summary:
- add a CLI agent input accumulator and route terminal input through
  submitted commands instead of per-keystroke detection
- collapse buffered output handling into a single service entrypoint
  that runs fallback startup, termination, and waiting in order
- add regression tests for submit-time connection and same-flush
  waiting detection

Rationale:
- keystroke-level detection could never recognize commands like
  "gemini" typed one character at a time
- waiting detection was split across separate calls, which made the
  connected-to-waiting transition fragile inside a single flush
- centralizing the pipeline keeps the existing detectors but makes the
  runtime flow easier to reason about and test

Tests:
- npx vitest run src/test/vitest/unit/terminals/TerminalIOCoordinator.test.ts src/test/vitest/unit/services/CliAgentDetectionService.test.ts src/test/vitest/unit/terminals/TerminalDataBufferManager.test.ts

commit.message:
fix(seo): remove [slug]/loading.tsx that hides H1 from crawlers (#830)

The loading.tsx at the [slug] level created an automatic Suspense
boundary around ALL storefront sub-pages. During SSR, Next.js rendered
StorefrontPageSkeleton as the initial HTML (no H1), with actual page
content only in RSC streaming payload scripts. Crawlers that don't
execute JavaScript (Ahrefs, some Googlebot modes) only saw the
skeleton — zero H1 tags.

The home page already has its own <Suspense> wrapper with the skeleton
fallback, so this file was redundant there and harmful for SEO on all
other pages (about, privacy, faq, contact, terms, etc.).

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
